description_long: This full-height turnstile is a robust, vertical access control barrier constructed primarily from metallic materials, featuring a rectangular frame with a flat, solid top enclosure. The central mechanism consists of multiple evenly spaced horizontal arms arranged in a radial pattern, forming a cylindrical rotating barrier that restricts or allows passage. Both sides of the frame are fitted with vertical bars, enhancing security and preventing bypassing the turnstile. A visible access control panel is mounted on one side, likely for card or biometric entry, indicating its use in secure urban environments such as subway stations, stadiums, or gated facilities. The metallic finish is smooth and slightly reflective, suggesting stainless steel construction for durability and weather resistance. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing security, controlled access, and resistance to tampering.
description: A tall, metallic full-height turnstile with a flat top, radial rotating arms, and vertical security bars, featuring an integrated access control panel. Its robust, secure design is suited for high-traffic urban entry points.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotating arms, flat top, and access control panel on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical security bars and the enclosed nature of the turnstile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rotating arms and the opposite side of the frame, without the access panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with vertical bars, mirroring the security features of the other side.
